task_completion.description: Open and review the project brief on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or file viewer is open displaying the contents of 'site_logistics_brief.txt' from the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Model a tower crane classified as an IfcTransportElement with PredefinedType CRANE.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D model of a crane is visible in the viewport. The outliner or object properties panel shows it is classified as 'IfcTransportElement' with a predefined type of 'CRANE'.

task_completion.description: Model a swing radius as an IfcBuildingElementProxy named 'Crane Swing Radius'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A circular or semi-circular 3D element representing the crane's swing radius is visible. The outliner or properties panel shows the object is named 'Crane Swing Radius' and classified as 'IfcBuildingElementProxy'.

task_completion.description: Create site hoarding consisting of at least 4 distinct elements forming a perimeter fence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least 4 distinct fence or hoarding elements are visible in the 3D viewport, arranged to form a perimeter around the site or building.

task_completion.description: Organize the crane, swing radius, and hoarding into an IfcGroup named 'Site Logistics'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The outliner, grouping, or spatial tree panel shows an 'IfcGroup' named 'Site Logistics' that contains the crane, swing radius, and hoarding elements.

task_completion.description: Save the updated IFC project to /home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_site_logistics.ifc.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, file explorer, or terminal confirmation showing the file 'fzk_site_logistics.ifc' being saved or existing in the '/home/ga/BIMProjects/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blender/Bonsai GUI to model and classify the elements.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the Blender 3D viewport, outliner, and Bonsai BIM properties panels, rather than just running a Python script in the terminal to generate the file.

integrity.description: The agent built the logistics plan around the actual FZK-Haus model.
integrity.visual_evidence: The pre-loaded FZK-Haus residential building is visible in the 3D viewport alongside the newly created crane and hoarding elements.

integrity.description: The agent incrementally created the logistics elements.
integrity.visual_evidence: The sequence of screenshots shows the step-by-step addition of the crane, radius, and hoarding, rather than the sudden appearance of a completed model.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using Bonsai (BlenderBIM) to author the IFC file. The specification document is located at '/home/ga/Desktop/site_logistics_brief.txt'. The final output must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_site_logistics.ifc'. The base model is the FZK-Haus sample model.
